title: Braya humilis
uses: Braya humilis, also known as dwarf alpine candytuft, has been used in traditional medicine for its potential antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's roots and leaves are said to have been used to treat various ailments, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. It is believed to have been used to reduce swelling and ease pain. However, more research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Braya humilis for medicinal purposes. In some cultures, it is still used today as a natural remedy for various health issues.
taxonomy: Braya humilis is a species of flowering plant in the family Brassicaceae. It is native to North America and is a low-growing, perennial herb. Braya humilis is classified as a member of the genus Braya, which is part of the Brassicaceae family. This classification is based on its morphological characteristics and genetic relationships with other plants in the family. The specific classification of Braya humilis is Braya humilis (Fernald) Polunin.

intro: Braya humilis, also known as low saxifrage, is a perennial plant species belonging to the saxifrage family (Brassicaceae) that is native to the Arctic tundra regions. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, and is used to treat various health issues such as arthritis, skin conditions, and respiratory problems. Bioactive constituents present in Braya humilis include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made from Braya humilis include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to harness its therapeutic benefits and promote overall health and well-being.
